name: Empanadas
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------Filling----------------------------------
1. Onion, chopped
2. Garlic, finely chopped (4 cloves)
3. Green pepper, seeded and chopped
4. Olive oil
5. Ground beef (8 ounces)
6. Cocoa powder (1 tablespoon)
7. Flour (1/2 teaspoon)
8. Cumin (1/2 teaspoon ground)
9. Paprika (1/2 teaspoon)
10. Dried oregano, crushed (1/2 teaspoon)
11. Salt and pepper to taste
12. Chili peppers, seeded and chopped
13. Canned crushed tomatoes (2 tablespoons)
14. Slivered almonds (2 tablespoons)
15. Chopped green olives (2 tablespoons)
16. Fresh parsley, chopped (2 tablespoons)
----------------------------------Pastry-----------------------------------
1. Sift the flour with a pinch of salt into a mixing bowl or food processor.
Mix once or twice. Rub in the margarine and shortening until the mixture
resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add liquid gradually, adding enough to bring
the pastry together into a ball. Wrap and chill for 20-30 minutes.
----------------------------------Assembly----------------------------------
1. Brown ground meat well and drain excess fat.
2. Cook onion until soft; add to meat.
3. Mix cocoa, flour, spices, oregano, salt, and pepper. Stir briefly.
4. Add chilies and tomatoes; cook slowly for 10-15 minutes.
5. Incorporate nuts, olives, and parsley (added with tomatoes).
----------------------------------Rolling and Sealing----------------------------------
1. Roll out pastry on a floured surface or through a pasta machine (#3 setting).
2. Cut into 4-inch rounds.
3. Place about 1½ tablespoons filling on each dough circle.
4. Fold over and press to seal; moisten edges with warm water if needed.
5. Crimp edges with a fork.
----------------------------------Baking----------------------------------
1. Prick several holes on top with a toothpick for steam release.
2. Brush empanadas with egg glaze.
3. Bake at 425°F for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Freezing Instructions:
Place frozen pastries on a baking sheet and brush with egg glaze.
Bake at 425°F for 30-35 minutes.
Makes 15 empanadas.
